competency_groups.title: Understanding Numbers and Real Number System
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Prove the irrationality.
Construct the square root spiral.

competency_groups.title: Algebraic Reasoning and Modelling
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; models real-life situations in the form of equations.
competencies: Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.
Define a polynomial.
Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.
Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.
Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.
Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a linear equation in two variables.

competency_groups.title: Sequences and Computational Thinking
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena using representations; uses computational thinking for pattern recognition.
competencies: Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.
Identify the pattern in a sequence and predict the next few terms.
Determine the recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.
Obtain the terms of sequence given its recursive and explicit rule.
Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP).
Determine the nth term of an AP.
Visualise an AP graphically.
Identify Geometric Progressions (GP).
Determine the nth term of a GP.
Visualise a GP graphically.
Analyse attributes of fractals using GP.

competency_groups.title: Visualisation and Decomposition of Identities
competency_groups.description: Uses computational thinking strategies such as decomposition to visualise identities.
competencies: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.
Determine the factors of algebraic expressions using identities.
Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.
Find simplified versions of rational expressions.

competency_groups.title: Linear Equations and Graphical Modelling
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena using representations; solves contextualised problems.
competencies: Understand the concept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a pair of linear equations.
Solve a pair of linear equations graphically.
Solve a pair of linear equations through substitution and elimination methods.
Determine the nature of solutions of a pair of linear equations.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry and Spatial Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Specifies locations using coordinates; applies computational thinking to model situations.
competencies: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.
Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.
Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.
Determine whether three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.
Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment using coordinates.

competency_groups.title: Axiomatic Structure and Constructions
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements using axioms; carries out geometric constructions.
competencies: Describe how geometry grew from practical needs of ancient civilisations.
Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.
State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.
Define parallelism of straight lines.
Justify simple constructions using the axioms.

competency_groups.title: Lines and Angles: Logical Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Follows proof by contradiction; applies computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.
competencies: Explain the notion of an angle and a ray.
Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.
State and apply linear pair theorem and its converse.
Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.
Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.

competency_groups.title: Triangle Congruence Theorems
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms; explains propositions and their converses.
competencies: Explain that a triangle is rigid.
Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.
Use SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions.
Prove basic properties of isosceles triangles.
Identify the converse of a given proposition.

competency_groups.title: Quadrilaterals and Symmetry
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems related to parallelograms; understands central symmetry.
competencies: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.
Prove the midpoint theorem and its converse.
Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons.

competency_groups.title: Circle Geometry and Properties
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems about circles; uses computational thinking to verify properties.
competencies: State the definition of a circle.
Explain meanings of chord, diameter, radius, arc, segment, sector.
Construct circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.
Prove relationships between chords, angles at centre, and cyclic quadrilaterals.

competency_groups.title: Mensuration: Area and Perimeter
competency_groups.description: Derives formulae for areas; uses computational thinking to break down shapes.
competencies: Define perimeter as the length around a shape.
Compute circumference of a circle and arc length.
Apply Heron's formula to compute area from sides.
Explain Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals.

competency_groups.title: Surface Area and Volume Calculations
competency_groups.description: Derives formulae for surface areas and volumes; uses computational thinking to compare shapes.
competencies: Compute the surface area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres.

competency_groups.title: Statistical Analysis and Data Interpretation
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendency; uses computational thinking to analyse real-life data.
competencies: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data.
Compute weighted average, mean, median, mode.

competency_groups.title: Probability and Randomness Modelling
competency_groups.description: Applies concepts from probability; uses computational thinking to model random experiments.
competencies: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe likelihood using probability scale.
Estimate empirical probability by analysing data.
Define and compute theoretical probability.
